NKJV.1: I, therefore, the prisoner of the Lord, beseech you to walk worthy of the calling with which you were called,
NKJV.2: with all lowliness and gentleness, with longsuffering, bearing with one another in love,
NKJV.3: endeavoring to keep the unity of the Spirit in the bond of peace.
NKJV.4: There is one body and one Spirit, just as you were called in one hope of your calling;
NKJV.5: one Lord, one faith, one baptism;
NKJV.6: one God and Father of all, who is above all, and through all, and in you all.
NKJV.7: But to each one of us grace was given according to the measure of Christ’s gift.
NKJV.8: Therefore He says:  “When He ascended on high, He led captivity captive, And gave gifts to men.”
NKJV.9: (Now this, “He ascended”—what does it mean but that He also first descended into the lower parts of the earth?
NKJV.10: He who descended is also the One who ascended far above all the heavens, that He might fill all things.)
NKJV.11: And He Himself gave some to be apostles, some prophets, some evangelists, and some pastors and teachers,
NKJV.12: for the equipping of the saints for the work of ministry, for the edifying of the body of Christ,
NKJV.13: till we all come to the unity of the faith and of the knowledge of the Son of God, to a perfect man, to the measure of the stature of the fullness of Christ;
NKJV.14: that we should no longer be children, tossed to and fro and carried about with every wind of doctrine, by the trickery of men, in the cunning craftiness of deceitful plotting,
NKJV.15: but, speaking the truth in love, may grow up in all things into Him who is the head—Christ—
NKJV.16: from whom the whole body, joined and knit together by what every joint supplies, according to the effective working by which every part does its share, causes growth of the body for the edifying of itself in love.
NKJV.17: This I say, therefore, and testify in the Lord, that you should no longer walk as the rest of the Gentiles walk, in the futility of their mind,
NKJV.18: having their understanding darkened, being alienated from the life of God, because of the ignorance that is in them, because of the blindness of their heart;
NKJV.19: who, being past feeling, have given themselves over to lewdness, to work all uncleanness with greediness.
NKJV.20: But you have not so learned Christ,
NKJV.21: if indeed you have heard Him and have been taught by Him, as the truth is in Jesus:
NKJV.22: that you put off, concerning your former conduct, the old man which grows corrupt according to the deceitful lusts,
NKJV.23: and be renewed in the spirit of your mind,
NKJV.24: and that you put on the new man which was created according to God, in true righteousness and holiness.
NKJV.25: Therefore, putting away lying, “Let each one of you speak truth with his neighbor,” for we are members of one another.
NKJV.26: “Be angry, and do not sin”: do not let the sun go down on your wrath,
NKJV.27: nor give place to the devil.
NKJV.28: Let him who stole steal no longer, but rather let him labor, working with his hands what is good, that he may have something to give him who has need.
NKJV.29: Let no corrupt word proceed out of your mouth, but what is good for necessary edification, that it may impart grace to the hearers.
NKJV.30: And do not grieve the Holy Spirit of God, by whom you were sealed for the day of redemption.
NKJV.31: Let all bitterness, wrath, anger, clamor, and evil speaking be put away from you, with all malice.
NKJV.32: And be kind to one another, tenderhearted, forgiving one another, even as God in Christ forgave you.

ESV.1: I therefore, a prisoner for the Lord, urge you to walk in a manner worthy of the calling to which you have been called,
ESV.2: with all humility and gentleness, with patience, bearing with one another in love,
ESV.3: eager to maintain the unity of the Spirit in the bond of peace.
ESV.4: There is one body and one Spirit — just as you were called to the one hope that belongs to your call —
ESV.5: one Lord, one faith, one baptism,
ESV.6: one God and Father of all, who is over all and through all and in all.
ESV.7: But grace was given to each one of us according to the measure of Christ's gift.
ESV.8: Therefore it says, “When he ascended on high he led a host of captives, and he gave gifts to men.”
ESV.9: (In saying, “He ascended,” what does it mean but that he had also descended into the lower regions, the earth?
ESV.10: He who descended is the one who also ascended far above all the heavens, that he might fill all things.)
ESV.11: And he gave the apostles, the prophets, the evangelists, the shepherds  and teachers,
ESV.12: to equip the saints for the work of ministry, for building up the body of Christ,
ESV.13: until we all attain to the unity of the faith and of the knowledge of the Son of God, to mature manhood,  to the measure of the stature of the fullness of Christ,
ESV.14: so that we may no longer be children, tossed to and fro by the waves and carried about by every wind of doctrine, by human cunning, by craftiness in deceitful schemes.
ESV.15: Rather, speaking the truth in love, we are to grow up in every way into him who is the head, into Christ,
ESV.16: from whom the whole body, joined and held together by every joint with which it is equipped, when each part is working properly, makes the body grow so that it builds itself up in love.
ESV.17: Now this I say and testify in the Lord, that you must no longer walk as the Gentiles do, in the futility of their minds.
ESV.18: They are darkened in their understanding, alienated from the life of God because of the ignorance that is in them, due to their hardness of heart.
ESV.19: They have become callous and have given themselves up to sensuality, greedy to practice every kind of impurity.
ESV.20: But that is not the way you learned Christ! —
ESV.21: assuming that you have heard about him and were taught in him, as the truth is in Jesus,
ESV.22: to put off your old self,  which belongs to your former manner of life and is corrupt through deceitful desires,
ESV.23: and to be renewed in the spirit of your minds,
ESV.24: and to put on the new self, created after the likeness of God in true righteousness and holiness.
ESV.25: Therefore, having put away falsehood, let each one of you speak the truth with his neighbor, for we are members one of another.
ESV.26: Be angry and do not sin; do not let the sun go down on your anger,
ESV.27: and give no opportunity to the devil.
ESV.28: Let the thief no longer steal, but rather let him labor, doing honest work with his own hands, so that he may have something to share with anyone in need.
ESV.29: Let no corrupting talk come out of your mouths, but only such as is good for building up, as fits the occasion, that it may give grace to those who hear.
ESV.30: And do not grieve the Holy Spirit of God, by whom you were sealed for the day of redemption.
ESV.31: Let all bitterness and wrath and anger and clamor and slander be put away from you, along with all malice.
ESV.32: Be kind to one another, tenderhearted, forgiving one another, as God in Christ forgave you.

NASB.1: Therefore I, the prisoner of the Lord, implore you to walk in a manner worthy of the calling with which you have been called,
NASB.2: with all humility and gentleness, with patience, showing tolerance for one another in love,
NASB.3: being diligent to preserve the unity of the Spirit in the bond of peace.
NASB.4: [There is] one body and one Spirit, just as also you were called in one hope of your calling;
NASB.5: one Lord, one faith, one baptism,
NASB.6: one God and Father of all who is over all and through all and in all.
NASB.7: But to each one of us grace was given according to the measure of Christ's gift.
NASB.8: Therefore it says, 'WHEN HE ASCENDED ON HIGH, HE LED CAPTIVE A HOST OF CAPTIVES, AND HE GAVE GIFTS TO MEN.'
NASB.9: (Now this [expression], 'He ascended,' what does it mean except that He also had descended into the lower parts of the earth?
NASB.10: He who descended is Himself also He who ascended far above all the heavens, so that He might fill all things.)
NASB.11: And He gave some [as] apostles, and some [as] prophets, and some [as] evangelists, and some [as] pastors and teachers,
NASB.12: for the equipping of the saints for the work of service, to the building up of the body of Christ;
NASB.13: until we all attain to the unity of the faith, and of the knowledge of the Son of God, to a mature man, to the measure of the stature which belongs to the fullness of Christ.
NASB.14: As a result, we are no longer to be children, tossed here and there by waves and carried about by every wind of doctrine, by the trickery of men, by craftiness in deceitful scheming;
NASB.15: but speaking the truth in love, we are to grow up in all [aspects] into Him who is the head, [even] Christ,
NASB.16: from whom the whole body, being fitted and held together by what every joint supplies, according to the proper working of each individual part, causes the growth of the body for the building up of itself in love.
NASB.17: So this I say, and affirm together with the Lord, that you walk no longer just as the Gentiles also walk, in the futility of their mind,
NASB.18: being darkened in their understanding, excluded from the life of God because of the ignorance that is in them, because of the hardness of their heart;
NASB.19: and they, having become callous, have given themselves over to sensuality for the practice of every kind of impurity with greediness.
NASB.20: But you did not learn Christ in this way,
NASB.21: if indeed you have heard Him and have been taught in Him, just as truth is in Jesus,
NASB.22: that, in reference to your former manner of life, you lay aside the old self, which is being corrupted in accordance with the lusts of deceit,
NASB.23: and that you be renewed in the spirit of your mind,
NASB.24: and put on the new self, which in [the likeness of] God has been created in righteousness and holiness of the truth.
NASB.25: Therefore, laying aside falsehood, SPEAK TRUTH EACH ONE [of you] WITH HIS NEIGHBOR, for we are members of one another.
NASB.26: BE ANGRY, AND [yet] DO NOT SIN; do not let the sun go down on your anger,
NASB.27: and do not give the devil an opportunity.
NASB.28: He who steals must steal no longer; but rather he must labor, performing with his own hands what is good, so that he will have [something] to share with one who has need.
NASB.29: Let no unwholesome word proceed from your mouth, but only such [a word] as is good for edification according to the need [of the moment], so that it will give grace to those who hear.
NASB.30: Do not grieve the Holy Spirit of God, by whom you were sealed for the day of redemption.
NASB.31: Let all bitterness and wrath and anger and clamor and slander be put away from you, along with all malice.
NASB.32: Be kind to one another, tender-hearted, forgiving each other, just as God in Christ also has forgiven you.

NLT.1: Therefore I, a prisoner for serving the Lord, beg you to lead a life worthy of your calling, for you have been called by God.
NLT.2: Always be humble and gentle. Be patient with each other, making allowance for each other’s faults because of your love.
NLT.3: Make every effort to keep yourselves united in the Spirit, binding yourselves together with peace.
NLT.4: For there is one body and one Spirit, just as you have been called to one glorious hope for the future.
NLT.5: There is one Lord, one faith, one baptism,
NLT.6:
one God and Father of all,
who is over all, in all, and living through all.
NLT.7: However, he has given each one of us a special gift through the generosity of Christ.
NLT.8:
That is why the Scriptures say, “When he ascended to the heights,
he led a crowd of captives
and gave gifts to his people.”
NLT.9: Notice that it says “he ascended.” This clearly means that Christ also descended to our lowly world.
NLT.10: And the same one who descended is the one who ascended higher than all the heavens, so that he might fill the entire universe with himself.
NLT.11: Now these are the gifts Christ gave to the church: the apostles, the prophets, the evangelists, and the pastors and teachers.
NLT.12: Their responsibility is to equip God’s people to do his work and build up the church, the body of Christ.
NLT.13: This will continue until we all come to such unity in our faith and knowledge of God’s Son that we will be mature in the Lord, measuring up to the full and complete standard of Christ.
NLT.14: Then we will no longer be immature like children. We won’t be tossed and blown about by every wind of new teaching. We will not be influenced when people try to trick us with lies so clever they sound like the truth.
NLT.15: Instead, we will speak the truth in love, growing in every way more and more like Christ, who is the head of his body, the church.
NLT.16: He makes the whole body fit together perfectly. As each part does its own special work, it helps the other parts grow, so that the whole body is healthy and growing and full of love.
NLT.17: With the Lord’s authority I say this: Live no longer as the Gentiles do, for they are hopelessly confused.
NLT.18: Their minds are full of darkness; they wander far from the life God gives because they have closed their minds and hardened their hearts against him.
NLT.19: They have no sense of shame. They live for lustful pleasure and eagerly practice every kind of impurity.
NLT.20: But that isn’t what you learned about Christ.
NLT.21: Since you have heard about Jesus and have learned the truth that comes from him,
NLT.22: throw off your old sinful nature and your former way of life, which is corrupted by lust and deception.
NLT.23: Instead, let the Spirit renew your thoughts and attitudes.
NLT.24: Put on your new nature, created to be like God — truly righteous and holy.
NLT.25: So stop telling lies. Let us tell our neighbors the truth, for we are all parts of the same body.
NLT.26: And “don’t sin by letting anger control you.” Don’t let the sun go down while you are still angry,
NLT.27: for anger gives a foothold to the devil.
NLT.28: If you are a thief, quit stealing. Instead, use your hands for good hard work, and then give generously to others in need.
NLT.29: Don’t use foul or abusive language. Let everything you say be good and helpful, so that your words will be an encouragement to those who hear them.
NLT.30: And do not bring sorrow to God’s Holy Spirit by the way you live. Remember, he has identified you as his own, guaranteeing that you will be saved on the day of redemption.
NLT.31: Get rid of all bitterness, rage, anger, harsh words, and slander, as well as all types of evil behavior.
NLT.32: Instead, be kind to each other, tenderhearted, forgiving one another, just as God through Christ has forgiven you.

WEB.1: I therefore, the prisoner in the Lord, beg you to walk worthily of the calling with which you were called,
WEB.2: with all lowliness and humility, with patience, bearing with one another in love;
WEB.3: being eager to keep the unity of the Spirit in the bond of peace.
WEB.4: There is one body, and one Spirit, even as you also were called in one hope of your calling;
WEB.5: one Lord, one faith, one baptism,
WEB.6: one God and Father of all, who is over all, and through all, and in us all.
WEB.7: But to each one of us was the grace given according to the measure of the gift of Christ.
WEB.8: Therefore he says, “When he ascended on high, he led captivity captive, and gave gifts to men.”
WEB.9: Now this, “He ascended”, what is it but that he also first descended into the lower parts of the earth?
WEB.10: He who descended is the one who also ascended far above all the heavens, that he might fill all things.
WEB.11: He gave some to be apostles; and some, prophets; and some, evangelists; and some, shepherds  and teachers;
WEB.12: for the perfecting of the saints, to the work of serving, to the building up of the body of Christ;
WEB.13: until we all attain to the unity of the faith, and of the knowledge of the Son of God, to a full grown man, to the measure of the stature of the fullness of Christ;
WEB.14: that we may no longer be children, tossed back and forth and carried about with every wind of doctrine, by the trickery of men, in craftiness, after the wiles of error;
WEB.15: but speaking truth in love, we may grow up in all things into him, who is the head, Christ;
WEB.16: from whom all the body, being fitted and knit together through that which every joint supplies, according to the working in measure of each individual part, makes the body increase to the building up of itself in love.
WEB.17: This I say therefore, and testify in the Lord, that you no longer walk as the rest of the Gentiles also walk, in the futility of their mind,
WEB.18: being darkened in their understanding, alienated from the life of God, because of the ignorance that is in them, because of the hardening of their hearts;
WEB.19: who having become callous gave themselves up to lust, to work all uncleanness with greediness.
WEB.20: But you did not learn Christ that way;
WEB.21: if indeed you heard him, and were taught in him, even as truth is in Jesus:
WEB.22: that you put away, as concerning your former way of life, the old man, that grows corrupt after the lusts of deceit;
WEB.23: and that you be renewed in the spirit of your mind,
WEB.24: and put on the new man, who in the likeness of God has been created in righteousness and holiness of truth.
WEB.25: Therefore putting away falsehood, speak truth each one with his neighbor. For we are members of one another.
WEB.26: “Be angry, and don’t sin.”  Don’t let the sun go down on your wrath,
WEB.27: and don’t give place to the devil.
WEB.28: Let him who stole steal no more; but rather let him labor, producing with his hands something that is good, that he may have something to give to him who has need.
WEB.29: Let no corrupt speech proceed out of your mouth, but only what is good for building others up as the need may be, that it may give grace to those who hear.
WEB.30: Don’t grieve the Holy Spirit of God, in whom you were sealed for the day of redemption.
WEB.31: Let all bitterness, wrath, anger, outcry, and slander, be put away from you, with all malice.
WEB.32: And be kind to one another, tender hearted, forgiving each other, just as God also in Christ forgave you.

CSB17.1: Therefore I, the prisoner in the Lord, <sup>ⓘ</sup> urge you to live worthy of the calling you have received,
CSB17.2: with all humility <sup>ⓙ</sup> and gentleness, with patience, <sup>ⓚ</sup> bearing <sup>ⓛ</sup> with one another in love, <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.3: making every effort to keep the unity <sup>ⓝ</sup> of the Spirit <sup>ⓐ</sup> through the bond of peace.
CSB17.4: There is one body and one Spirit <sup>ⓑ</sup>  — just as you were called to one hope <sup>[1]</sup> <sup>ⓒ</sup> at your calling —
CSB17.5: one Lord, <sup>ⓓ</sup> one faith, <sup>ⓔ</sup> one baptism,
CSB17.6: one God <sup>ⓕ</sup> and Father <sup>ⓖ</sup> of all, who is above all and through all and in all. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.7: Now grace was given to each one of us <sup>ⓘ</sup> according to the measure of Christ’s gift.
CSB17.8:
For it says: When he ascended ⓙ on high,
he took the captives captive;
he gave gifts to people. [2] ⓚ
CSB17.9: But what does “he ascended” mean except that he <sup>[3]</sup> also descended to the lower parts of the earth? <sup>[4]</sup> <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.10: The one who descended is also the one who ascended far above all the heavens, <sup>ⓜ</sup> to fill all things. <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.11: And he himself gave some to be apostles, some prophets, some evangelists, some pastors and teachers,
CSB17.12: equipping the saints for the work of ministry, to build up the body of Christ, <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.13: until we all reach unity in the faith and in the knowledge of God’s Son, <sup>ⓑ</sup> growing into maturity with a stature <sup>ⓒ</sup> measured by Christ’s fullness.
CSB17.14: Then we will no longer be little children, tossed by the waves and blown around by every wind of teaching, <sup>ⓓ</sup> by human cunning with cleverness in the techniques of deceit.
CSB17.15: But speaking the truth in love, let us grow in every way into him who is the head <sup>ⓔ</sup>  — Christ.
CSB17.16: From him the whole body, fitted and knit together <sup>ⓕ</sup> by every supporting ligament, promotes the growth <sup>ⓖ</sup> of the body for building up itself in love by the proper working of each individual part.
CSB17.17: Therefore, I say this and testify in the Lord: You should no longer live as the Gentiles live, in the futility of their thoughts.
CSB17.18: They are darkened in their understanding, excluded from the life of God, because of the ignorance that is in them and because <sup>[5]</sup> of the hardness of their hearts.
CSB17.19: They became callous and gave themselves over to promiscuity for the practice of every kind of impurity <sup>ⓗ</sup> with a desire for more and more. <sup>[6]</sup>
CSB17.20: But that is not how you came to know Christ,
CSB17.21: assuming you heard about him and were taught by him, as the truth <sup>ⓘ</sup> is in Jesus,
CSB17.22: to take off <sup>[7]</sup> <sup>ⓙ</sup> your former way of life, <sup>ⓚ</sup> the old self <sup>ⓛ</sup> that is corrupted by deceitful desires,
CSB17.23: to be renewed <sup>[8]</sup> in the spirit <sup>ⓜ</sup> of your minds,
CSB17.24: and to put on <sup>[9]</sup> <sup>ⓝ</sup> the new self, the one created <sup>ⓐ</sup> according to God’s likeness <sup>ⓑ</sup> in righteousness and purity of the truth.
CSB17.25: Therefore, putting away ⓒ lying, speak the truth, each one to his neighbor, [10] ⓓ because we are members of one another.
CSB17.26: Be angry and do not sin. [11] ⓔ Don’t let the sun go down on your anger,
CSB17.27: and don’t give the devil an opportunity.
CSB17.28: Let the thief no longer steal. Instead, he is to do honest work with his own hands, so that he has something to share <sup>ⓕ</sup> with anyone in need.
CSB17.29: No foul language should come from your mouth, but only what is good for building up someone in need, <sup>[12]</sup> so that it gives grace to those who hear.
CSB17.30: And don’t grieve God’s Holy Spirit. <sup>ⓖ</sup> You were sealed by him <sup>[13]</sup> for the day of redemption. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.31: Let all bitterness, anger and wrath, shouting and slander be removed from you, along with all malice.
CSB17.32: And be kind <sup>ⓘ</sup> and compassionate <sup>ⓙ</sup> to one another, forgiving <sup>ⓚ</sup> one another, just as God also forgave you <sup>[14]</sup> in Christ.

AMP.1: So I, the prisoner for the Lord, appeal to you to live a life worthy of the calling to which you have been called [that is, to live a life that exhibits godly character, moral courage, personal integrity, and mature behavior—a life that expresses gratitude to God for your salvation],
AMP.2: with all humility [forsaking self-righteousness], and gentleness [maintaining self-control], with patience, bearing with one another in [unselfish] love.
AMP.3: Make every effort to keep the oneness of the Spirit in the bond of peace [each individual working together to make the whole successful].
AMP.4: There is one body [of believers] and one Spirit—just as you were called to one hope when called [to salvation]—
AMP.5: one Lord, one faith, one baptism,
AMP.6: one God and Father of us all who is [sovereign] over all and [working] through all and [living] in all.
AMP.7: Yet grace [God's undeserved favor] was given to each one of us [not indiscriminately, but in different ways] in proportion to the measure of Christ's [rich and abundant] gift.
AMP.8:
Therefore it says,
"When He ascended on high,
He led captivity captive,
And He bestowed gifts on men."
AMP.9: (Now this expression, "He ascended," what does it mean except that He also had previously descended [from the heights of heaven] into the lower parts of the earth?
AMP.10: He who descended is the very same as He who also has ascended high above all the heavens, that He [His presence] might fill all things [that is, the whole universe]).
AMP.11: And [His gifts to the church were varied and] He Himself appointed some as apostles [special messengers, representatives], some as prophets [who speak a new message from God to the people], some as evangelists [who spread the good news of salvation], and some as pastors and teachers [to shepherd and guide and instruct],
AMP.12: [and He did this] to fully equip and perfect the saints (God's people) for works of service, to build up the body of Christ [the church];
AMP.13: until we all reach oneness in the faith and in the knowledge of the Son of God, [growing spiritually] to become a mature believer, reaching to the measure of the fullness of Christ [manifesting His spiritual completeness and exercising our spiritual gifts in unity].
AMP.14: So that we are no longer children [spiritually immature], tossed back and forth [like ships on a stormy sea] and carried about by every wind of [shifting] doctrine, by the cunning and trickery of [unscrupulous] men, by the deceitful scheming of people ready to do anything [for personal profit].
AMP.15: But speaking the truth in love [in all things—both our speech and our lives expressing His truth], let us grow up in all things into Him [following His example] who is the Head—Christ.
AMP.16: From Him the whole body [the church, in all its various parts], joined and knitted firmly together by what every joint supplies, when each part is working properly, causes the body to grow and mature, building itself up in [unselfish] love.
AMP.17: So this I say, and solemnly affirm together with the Lord [as in His presence], that you must no longer live as the [unbelieving] Gentiles live, in the futility of their minds [and in the foolishness and emptiness of their souls],
AMP.18: for their [moral] understanding is darkened and their reasoning is clouded; [they are] alienated and self-banished from the life of God [with no share in it; this is] because of the [willful] ignorance and spiritual blindness that is [deep-seated] within them, because of the hardness and insensitivity of their heart.
AMP.19: And they, [the ungodly in their spiritual apathy], having become callous and unfeeling, have given themselves over [as prey] to unbridled sensuality, eagerly craving the practice of every kind of impurity [that their desires may demand].
AMP.20: But you did not learn Christ in this way!
AMP.21: If in fact you have [really] heard Him and have been taught by Him, just as truth is in Jesus [revealed in His life and personified in Him],
AMP.22: that, regarding your previous way of life, you put off your old self [completely discard your former nature], which is being corrupted through deceitful desires,
AMP.23: and be continually renewed in the spirit of your mind [having a fresh, untarnished mental and spiritual attitude],
AMP.24: and put on the new self [the regenerated and renewed nature], created in God's image, [godlike] in the righteousness and holiness of the truth [living in a way that expresses to God your gratitude for your salvation].
AMP.25: Therefore, rejecting all falsehood [whether lying, defrauding, telling half-truths, spreading rumors, any such as these], speak truth each one with his neighbor, for we are all parts of one another [and we are all parts of the body of Christ].
AMP.26: Be angry [at sin—at immorality, at injustice, at ungodly behavior], yet do not sin; do not let your anger [cause you shame, nor allow it to] last until the sun goes down.
AMP.27: And do not give the devil an opportunity [to lead you into sin by holding a grudge, or nurturing anger, or harboring resentment, or cultivating bitterness].
AMP.28: The thief [who has become a believer] must no longer steal, but instead he must work hard [making an honest living], producing that which is good with his own hands, so that he will have something to share with those in need.
AMP.29: Do not let unwholesome [foul, profane, worthless, vulgar] words ever come out of your mouth, but only such speech as is good for building up others, according to the need and the occasion, so that it will be a blessing to those who hear [you speak].
AMP.30: And do not grieve the Holy Spirit of God [but seek to please Him], by whom you were sealed and marked [branded as God's own] for the day of redemption [the final deliverance from the consequences of sin].
AMP.31: Let all bitterness and wrath and anger and clamor [perpetual animosity, resentment, strife, fault-finding] and slander be put away from you, along with every kind of malice [all spitefulness, verbal abuse, malevolence].
AMP.32: Be kind and helpful to one another, tender-hearted [compassionate, understanding], forgiving one another [readily and freely], just as God in Christ also forgave you.

MSG.1: In light of all this, here's what I want you to do. While I'm locked up here, a prisoner for the Master, I want you to get out there and walk—better yet, run!—on the road God called you to travel. I don't want any of you sitting around on your hands. I don't want anyone strolling off, down some path that goes nowhere.
MSG.2: And mark that you do this with humility and discipline—not in fits and starts, but steadily, pouring yourselves out for each other in acts of love,
MSG.3: alert at noticing differences and quick at mending fences.
MSG.4: You were all called to travel on the same road and in the same direction, so stay together, both outwardly and inwardly.
MSG.5: You have one Master, one faith, one baptism,
MSG.6: one God and Father of all, who rules over all, works through all, and is present in all. Everything you are and think and do is permeated with Oneness.
MSG.7: But that doesn't mean you should all look and speak and act the same. Out of the generosity of Christ, each of us is given his own gift.
MSG.8: The text for this is, He climbed the high mountain, He captured the enemy and seized the booty, He handed it all out in gifts to the people.
MSG.9: It's true, is it not, that the One who climbed up also climbed down, down to the valley of earth?
MSG.10: And the One who climbed down is the One who climbed back up, up to highest heaven. He handed out gifts above and below, filled heaven with his gifts,
MSG.11: filled earth with his gifts. He handed out gifts of apostle, prophet, evangelist, and pastor-teacher
MSG.12: to train Christians in skilled servant work, working within Christ's body, the church,
MSG.13: until we're all moving rhythmically and easily with each other, efficient and graceful in response to God's Son, fully mature adults, fully developed within and without, fully alive like Christ.
MSG.14: No prolonged infancies among us, please. We'll not tolerate babes in the woods, small children who are an easy mark for impostors.
MSG.15: God wants us to grow up, to know the whole truth and tell it in love—like Christ in everything. We take our lead from Christ, who is the source of everything we do.
MSG.16: He keeps us in step with each other. His very breath and blood flow through us, nourishing us so that we will grow up healthy in God, robust in love.
MSG.17: And so I insist—and God backs me up on this—that there be no going along with the crowd, the empty-headed, mindless crowd.
MSG.18: They've refused for so long to deal with God that they've lost touch not only with God but with reality itself.
MSG.19: They can't think straight anymore. Feeling no pain, they let themselves go in sexual obsession, addicted to every sort of perversion.
MSG.20: But that's no life for you. You learned Christ!
MSG.21: My assumption is that you have paid careful attention to him, been well instructed in the truth precisely as we have it in Jesus.
MSG.22: Since, then, we do not have the excuse of ignorance, everything—and I do mean everything—connected with that old way of life has to go. It's rotten through and through. Get rid of it! And then take on an entirely new way of life—a God-fashioned life,
MSG.23: a life renewed from the inside
MSG.24: and working itself into your conduct as God accurately reproduces his character in you.
MSG.25: What this adds up to, then, is this: no more lies, no more pretense. Tell your neighbor the truth. In Christ's body we're all connected to each other, after all. When you lie to others, you end up lying to yourself.
MSG.26: Go ahead and be angry. You do well to be angry—but don't use your anger as fuel for revenge. And don't stay angry. Don't go to bed angry.
MSG.27: Don't give the Devil that kind of foothold in your life.
MSG.28: Did you used to make ends meet by stealing? Well, no more! Get an honest job so that you can help others who can't work.
MSG.29: Watch the way you talk. Let nothing foul or dirty come out of your mouth. Say only what helps, each word a gift.
MSG.30: Don't grieve God. Don't break his heart. His Holy Spirit, moving and breathing in you, is the most intimate part of your life, making you fit for himself. Don't take such a gift for granted.
MSG.31: Make a clean break with all cutting, backbiting, profane talk.
MSG.32: Be gentle with one another, sensitive. Forgive one another as quickly and thoroughly as God in Christ forgave you.

NIV.1:
Unity in the Body of Christ
As a prisoner for the Lord, then, I urge you to live a life worthy of the calling you have received.
NIV.2: Be completely humble and gentle; be patient, bearing with one another in love.
NIV.3: Make every effort to keep the unity of the Spirit through the bond of peace.
NIV.4: There is one body and one Spirit—just as you were called to one hope when you were called—
NIV.5: one Lord, one faith, one baptism;
NIV.6: one God and Father of all, who is over all and through all and in all.
NIV.7: But to each one of us grace has been given as Christ apportioned it.
NIV.8:
This is why it says:
“When he ascended on high,
he led captives in his train
and gave gifts to men.”
NIV.9: (What does “he ascended” mean except that he also descended to the lower, earthly regions?
NIV.10: He who descended is the very one who ascended higher than all the heavens, in order to fill the whole universe.)
NIV.11: It was he who gave some to be apostles, some to be prophets, some to be evangelists, and some to be pastors and teachers,
NIV.12: to prepare God’s people for works of service, so that the body of Christ may be built up
NIV.13: until we all reach unity in the faith and in the knowledge of the Son of God and become mature, attaining to the whole measure of the fullness of Christ.
NIV.14: Then we will no longer be infants, tossed back and forth by the waves, and blown here and there by every wind of teaching and by the cunning and craftiness of men in their deceitful scheming.
NIV.15: Instead, speaking the truth in love, we will in all things grow up into him who is the Head, that is, Christ.
NIV.16: From him the whole body, joined and held together by every supporting ligament, grows and builds itself up in love, as each part does its work.
NIV.17:
Living as Children of Light
So I tell you this, and insist on it in the Lord, that you must no longer live as the Gentiles do, in the futility of their thinking.
NIV.18: They are darkened in their understanding and separated from the life of God because of the ignorance that is in them due to the hardening of their hearts.
NIV.19: Having lost all sensitivity, they have given themselves over to sensuality so as to indulge in every kind of impurity, with a continual lust for more.
NIV.20: You, however, did not come to know Christ that way.
NIV.21: Surely you heard of him and were taught in him in accordance with the truth that is in Jesus.
NIV.22: You were taught, with regard to your former way of life, to put off your old self, which is being corrupted by its deceitful desires;
NIV.23: to be made new in the attitude of your minds;
NIV.24: and to put on the new self, created to be like God in true righteousness and holiness.
NIV.25: Therefore each of you must put off falsehood and speak truthfully to his neighbor, for we are all members of one body.
NIV.26: “In your anger do not sin”: Do not let the sun go down while you are still angry,
NIV.27: and do not give the devil a foothold.
NIV.28: He who has been stealing must steal no longer, but must work, doing something useful with his own hands, that he may have something to share with those in need.
NIV.29: Do not let any unwholesome talk come out of your mouths, but only what is helpful for building others up according to their needs, that it may benefit those who listen.
NIV.30: And do not grieve the Holy Spirit of God, with whom you were sealed for the day of redemption.
NIV.31: Get rid of all bitterness, rage and anger, brawling and slander, along with every form of malice.
NIV.32: Be kind and compassionate to one another, forgiving each other, just as in Christ God forgave you.

NRSVCE.1: I therefore, the prisoner in the Lord, beg you to lead a life worthy of the calling to which you have been called,
NRSVCE.2: with all humility and gentleness, with patience, bearing with one another in love,
NRSVCE.3: making every effort to maintain the unity of the Spirit in the bond of peace.
NRSVCE.4: There is one body and one Spirit, just as you were called to the one hope of your calling,
NRSVCE.5: one Lord, one faith, one baptism,
NRSVCE.6: one God and Father of all, who is above all and through all and in all.
NRSVCE.7: But each of us was given grace according to the measure of Christ’s gift.
NRSVCE.8:
Therefore it is said, “When he ascended on high he made captivity itself a captive;
he gave gifts to his people.”
NRSVCE.9: (When it says, “He ascended,” what does it mean but that he had also descended into the lower parts of the earth?
NRSVCE.10: He who descended is the same one who ascended far above all the heavens, so that he might fill all things.)
NRSVCE.11: The gifts he gave were that some would be apostles, some prophets, some evangelists, some pastors and teachers,
NRSVCE.12: to equip the saints for the work of ministry, for building up the body of Christ,
NRSVCE.13: until all of us come to the unity of the faith and of the knowledge of the Son of God, to maturity, to the measure of the full stature of Christ.
NRSVCE.14: We must no longer be children, tossed to and fro and blown about by every wind of doctrine, by people’s trickery, by their craftiness in deceitful scheming.
NRSVCE.15: But speaking the truth in love, we must grow up in every way into him who is the head, into Christ,
NRSVCE.16: from whom the whole body, joined and knit together by every ligament with which it is equipped, as each part is working properly, promotes the body’s growth in building itself up in love.
NRSVCE.17: Now this I affirm and insist on in the Lord: you must no longer live as the Gentiles live, in the futility of their minds.
NRSVCE.18: They are darkened in their understanding, alienated from the life of God because of their ignorance and hardness of heart.
NRSVCE.19: They have lost all sensitivity and have abandoned themselves to licentiousness, greedy to practice every kind of impurity.
NRSVCE.20: That is not the way you learned Christ!
NRSVCE.21: For surely you have heard about him and were taught in him, as truth is in Jesus.
NRSVCE.22: You were taught to put away your former way of life, your old self, corrupt and deluded by its lusts,
NRSVCE.23: and to be renewed in the spirit of your minds,
NRSVCE.24: and to clothe yourselves with the new self, created according to the likeness of God in true righteousness and holiness.
NRSVCE.25: So then, putting away falsehood, let all of us speak the truth to our neighbors, for we are members of one another.
NRSVCE.26: Be angry but do not sin; do not let the sun go down on your anger,
NRSVCE.27: and do not make room for the devil.
NRSVCE.28: Thieves must give up stealing; rather let them labor and work honestly with their own hands, so as to have something to share with the needy.
NRSVCE.29: Let no evil talk come out of your mouths, but only what is useful for building up, as there is need, so that your words may give grace to those who hear.
NRSVCE.30: And do not grieve the Holy Spirit of God, with which you were marked with a seal for the day of redemption.
NRSVCE.31: Put away from you all bitterness and wrath and anger and wrangling and slander, together with all malice,
NRSVCE.32: and be kind to one another, tenderhearted, forgiving one another, as God in Christ has forgiven you.

NET.1: I, therefore, the prisoner for the Lord, urge you to live worthily of the calling with which you have been called,
NET.2: with all humility and gentleness, with patience, bearing with one another in love,
NET.3: making every effort to keep the unity of the Spirit in the bond of peace.
NET.4: There is one body and one Spirit, just as you too were called to the one hope of your calling,
NET.5: one Lord, one faith, one baptism,
NET.6: one God and Father of all, who is over all and through all and in all.
NET.7: But to each one of us grace was given according to the measure of the gift of Christ.
NET.8: Therefore it says, “When he ascended on high he captured captives; he gave gifts to men.”
NET.9: Now what is the meaning of “he ascended,” except that he also descended to the lower regions, namely, the earth?
NET.10: He, the very one who descended, is also the one who ascended above all the heavens, in order to fill all things.
NET.11: It was he who gave some as apostles, some as prophets, some as evangelists, and some as pastors and teachers,
NET.12: to equip the saints for the work of ministry, that is, to build up the body of Christ,
NET.13: until we all attain to the unity of the faith and of the knowledge of the Son of God — a mature person, attaining to the measure of Christ’s full stature.
NET.14: So we are no longer to be children, tossed back and forth by waves and carried about by every wind of teaching by the trickery of people who craftily carry out their deceitful schemes.
NET.15: But practicing the truth in love, we will in all things grow up into Christ, who is the head.
NET.16: From him the whole body grows, fitted and held together through every supporting ligament. As each one does its part, the body grows in love.
NET.17: So I say this, and insist in the Lord, that you no longer live as the Gentiles do, in the futility of their thinking.
NET.18: They are darkened in their understanding, being alienated from the life of God because of the ignorance that is in them due to the hardness of their hearts.
NET.19: Because they are callous, they have given themselves over to indecency for the practice of every kind of impurity with greediness.
NET.20: But you did not learn about Christ like this,
NET.21: if indeed you heard about him and were taught in him, just as the truth is in Jesus.
NET.22: You were taught with reference to your former way of life to lay aside the old man who is being corrupted in accordance with deceitful desires,
NET.23: to be renewed in the spirit of your mind,
NET.24: and to put on the new man who has been created in God’s image — in righteousness and holiness that comes from truth.
NET.25: Therefore, having laid aside falsehood, each one of you speak the truth with his neighbor, for we are members of one another.
NET.26: Be angry and do not sin; do not let the sun go down on the cause of your anger.
NET.27: Do not give the devil an opportunity.
NET.28: The one who steals must steal no longer; rather he must labor, doing good with his own hands, so that he may have something to share with the one who has need.
NET.29: You must let no unwholesome word come out of your mouth, but only what is beneficial for the building up of the one in need, that it may give grace to those who hear.
NET.30: And do not grieve the Holy Spirit of God, by whom you were sealed for the day of redemption.
NET.31: You must put away every kind of bitterness, anger, wrath, quarreling, and evil, slanderous talk.
NET.32: Instead, be kind to one another, compassionate, forgiving one another, just as God in Christ also forgave you.
